pr.title: Matched THPDec_803302EC function in the THPDec.c file
pr.author: asylum14

summary: Matched previously-unmatched THP decoder functions in the external Dolphin THP decoder code. The PR title/body emphasizes THPDec_803302EC and explicitly says its purpose was unclear, while the decomp.dev bot reported two new 100% matches in main/dolphin/thp/THPDec: THPDec_803302EC (+216 bytes) and THPDec_803300E0 (+120 bytes). The change also exposed both symbols in dolphin/thp/thp.h and made broad formatting/layout edits in THPDec.c that likely supported matching.

key_files.role: Implemented matched THPDec_803302EC and THPDec_803300E0 and adjusted formatting/casts/line layout throughout THP decoder code.
Added public prototypes for s32 THPDec_803302EC(u8** data) and void THPDec_803300E0(u32* data).

what_changed: Added THPDec_803302EC(u8** data), which reads a big-endian 16-bit count from the current byte pointer and advances the caller's data pointer count times before returning 0.
Added THPDec_803300E0(u32* data), which treats the argument as a u32 buffer, aligns buffer[577] upward to 32 bytes, advances it by 2048, and fills offset entries in a small loop.
Updated dolphin/thp/thp.h with prototypes for THPDec_803302EC and THPDec_803300E0.
Reordered includes so <dolphin.h> appears before <dolphin/thp/thp.h> in THPDec.c.

Made many whitespace, cast-spacing, line-wrap, and brace-layout changes across THPDec.c, including constant arrays, pointer casts, THPROUNDUP, THPVideoDecode, Huffman table handling, IDCT helpers, and MCU-row decompression code.
The decomp.dev report recorded matched code increasing by +336 bytes and listed THPDec_803302EC and THPDec_803300E0 as new 100.00% matches.
smart_moves: Kept the newly matched functions address-style named, which is appropriate when the PR author stated the purpose of THPDec_803302EC was unclear.
Added header prototypes for the new externally visible THPDec_* symbols instead of leaving them only as source-local definitions.
Used simple width-specific Dolphin types such as u8, u16, u32, and s32, matching surrounding THP decoder style and making byte/word behavior explicit.
For THPDec_803302EC, preserved pointer-to-pointer semantics so the function can update the caller's stream cursor directly.
For THPDec_803300E0, expressed the 32-byte alignment as (buffer[577] + 31) & 0xFFFFFFE0, a common decomp form for matching PowerPC alignment code.
decomp_lessons: When a function's purpose is unknown, prefer an address-derived name such as THPDec_803302EC and document uncertainty rather than inventing a semantic name.
Small SDK helper routines may be matched by faithfully modeling pointer cursor movement and alignment math rather than trying to over-abstract the code.
Header updates matter for matched external SDK functions: if a THPDec_* function is not static and may be referenced elsewhere, add a prototype in the corresponding public header.
Broad formatting changes can accompany matching, but future reviewers/searchers should distinguish actual new matched logic from style churn.
The bot report is useful evidence for matching outcome: here it confirms both THPDec_803302EC and THPDec_803300E0 reached 100.00%, even though the PR title focused only on THPDec_803302EC.
naming_conventions: Unknown THP decoder functions retained address-based names with the module prefix: THPDec_803302EC and THPDec_803300E0.
Existing internal THP helpers use double-underscore THP prefixes, e.g. __THPPrepBitStream, __THPReadFrameHeader, __THPHuffDecodeTab.
Public or externally declared THP API-style functions use THP-prefixed names without the double underscore, e.g. THPVideoDecode, THPInit, THPDec_803302EC.
Local variables in the added functions are short and mechanical, such as ptr, high, low, count, val, offset, and buffer, reflecting uncertain semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used big-endian byte composition from ptr[0] and ptr[1] into a u16 count: (high << 8) | low.
Advanced a u8** stream cursor by incrementing (*data)++ inside a counted loop, preserving caller-visible pointer mutation.
Used explicit 32-byte alignment masking with 0xFFFFFFE0 rather than a helper macro in THPDec_803300E0.
Populated fixed buffer offsets with constants 128, 256, 384, 512, 1024, and 2048, likely matching literal arithmetic in the target function.
Adjusted cast spacing and line breaks across THPDec.c; such layout changes can affect matching with older compiler/decomp style constraints or at least reduce diff friction around matched code.
Kept existing inline assembly regions and labels in __THPHuffDecodeTab intact while only reformatting surrounding C expressions.

review_feedback.feedback: The author stated: 'Unclear what the purpose of the function is.' This is evidence that no semantic rename was justified.
Matched code increased by +336 bytes; THPDec_803302EC matched +216 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%, and THPDec_803300E0 matched +120 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find callers or references of THPDec_803302EC to determine whether it skips a THP/JPEG segment length or another length-prefixed block.
Find callers or references of THPDec_803300E0 to determine the semantic name for its buffer offset table setup.
Compare THPDec_803300E0 against Nintendo Dolphin SDK THP decoder sources, if available, for an official function name.
Audit whether the broad THPDec.c formatting changes were required for matching or were incidental style cleanup.
